title: Duplicated signature header parsing logic
description:
src/signatures.rs:split_signature_params (lines 128-159) and src/server.rs:extract_key_id_from_sig (lines 1212-1250) contain near-identical state machines for parsing HTTP Signature header quoted-string params (tracking in_quotes, escape_next, splitting on commas). The only difference is that one collects all params and the other extracts keyId.

Impact: Maintenance risk — a bug fix in one parser won't be applied to the other. This is not a style nit; the two implementations could diverge on edge cases (e.g., handling of escaped quotes or empty fields), producing inconsistent security behavior in signature verification.

Fix: Extract a shared split_signature_params function (already exists in signatures.rs) and call it from extract_key_id_from_sig, or move both into signatures.rs and make extract_key_id_from_sig a thin wrapper.
close_reason: Removed duplicate extract_key_id_from_sig, now using signatures::parse_signature_header
